as PDF Printable version In other projects Wikidata item Appearance move to sidebar hide From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia American business executive John Rau was an American business executive. 1 2 3 A four-time CEO, he advised corporate leaders and served as the Poling Chair of Business and Government Leadership at Indiana University s Kelley School of Business . 4 5 6 Education edit Rau attended Boston College as an undergraduate. 7 He received a Master of Business Administration from Harvard University , where he was the Goldman Sachs Fellow. 8 Career edit Rau served as a chief executive officer four times. 9 At the age of 35, he was the youngest of the top 100 bank presidents in the United States. 10 Rau was CEO of Exchange Bank, selling it to ABN Bank for 450 million in 1989. 11 ABN's LaSalle Bank then appointed him CEO, with Rau stepping down in 1991, but staying on ABN's North America board until it sold LaSalle to Bank of America for 23 billion in 2007. 12 After LaSalle Bank, Rau became the dean of Indiana University's Kelley School of Business. 13 He was also a visiting scholar at Northwestern University s Kellogg School of Management from 1992 to 1993, and counsel to the firm of McKinsey Company . 14 15 16 In 1996, Rau became CEO of Chicago Title Trust, selling it to Fidelity National in 2000 for a stock-cash value of 1.2 billion. 17 18 19 In 2002, he was named CEO of the Chicago, Illinois-based private investment holding company Miami Corp., serving as CEO until his retirement in 2023. 20 In September 2024, Rau became the Poling Chair of Business and Government Leadership at the Kelley School. 21 He was an 11-time corporate director, including six times as lead or chair, and a Forbes contributor. 22 Publications edit Secrets from the Search
